title: Docker API incompatibility: invalid --security-opt: "label:disable"
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

In docker CLI it is possible to use the `:` character as assignment operator or at least `label:disable` as valid substitute for `label=disable`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
podman run --rm --security-opt "label:disable" quay.io/sheogorath/docker-socket-proxy:2.3
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: invalid --security-opt 1: "label:disable"
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

This should work to widen the docker API compatibility.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.16.3
Built:        Wed May 12 21:27:59 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: That format was deprecated by Docker years ago.
Ah, I see. In that case I'll just update the format, so not a problem.
